name: pr_tms_diamond
drl:
rule A when R1(v > 0) then insertLogical(new Mid("m")); end
rule B when R2(v > 0) then insertLogical(new Mid("m")); end
rule C when Mid(k == "m") then insertLogical(new Leaf("l")); end
_finding: The DIAMOND justification graph (external review round 16/17, his clean composition pinned): two roots value-merge onto one Mid, Mid justifies Leaf. Deleting ONE root leaves both tiers in place (C1-style partial break stays flicker-free one tier down - the D-236 boundary generalizes); deleting the SECOND cascades Mid then Leaf. Multi-justifier survival composes with downstream cascade.
